title: Identity, Permissions, Ownership, umask, and ACLs · Terminal Trace: Umask Defaults
summary: Create a shared operations directory that grants required access without world-writable shortcuts or unexplained privilege escalation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux umask defaults increment in a least-privilege access matrix and permission lab. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive default file and directory modes from requested modes and umask without treating umask as subtraction.
Translate symbolic and octal chmod operations while preserving unrelated bits and verifying the result.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Umask Defaults
steps.instruction:
theory case — Directory /srv/reports is owned by learner:lab with mode 2770 and umask 0027. A lab-group writer and outside reader attempt create, list, delete, and execute operations; derive each result before changing ACLs.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Umask Defaults contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern

A lab-group writer and outside reader attempt create, list, delete, and execute operations; derive each result before changing ACLs. A defensible solution applies Umask Defaults, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a least-privilege access matrix and permission lab.

content.code:
id
umask
stat -c 'mode=%a owner=%U group=%G path=%n' reports
# Derive directory create/delete behavior before any chmod or ACL change.
